methodology: #461 logit-margin locus (teacher-forced M=8 prompt_logprobs vs M=1 AR, no greedy cascade); enforce_eager (no cudagraph confound); same locus surgical-357 + #500 byteexact were certified on
mechanism_note: enable_batch_invariant_mode() patches aten mm/addmm/matmul/linear/bmm/softmax/mean only; it does NOT touch triton_unified_attention.is_batch_invariant. int4 production matmuls go through torch.ops._C.marlin_gemm (custom op, NOT aten::mm) so the tax cannot touch them.

summary.run_prefix: LOCAL analysis_only (no HF job / no run_request.json / no --launch). Evidence: research/validity/byteexact399_literal_matmul/causation_census/ (3-arm #461 margin census) + gemm_tax_repro.json (#501 microbench repro, wandb j6dxoe28)
summary.failure_logs: none (0 tracebacks, 0 fatal markers, 3/3 ARM_DONE across all arms)
summary.ppl_note: ppl 2.3767 is the byteexact-399 anchor (#496/#500); int4 quant/precache/lm_head-prune all UNCHANGED -> PPL identical, not re-measured in this analysis-only flip census

tps_cost_of_full_tax.source: #501 microbench (reproduced first-party, wandb j6dxoe28): surgical(345.22, 2D byte-exact attn + STOCK Marlin) - full_flag(212.28, VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1) = 132.94
tps_cost_of_full_tax.byteexact_full_env_tps_cost_est: ~188 (byteexact 399.75 -> ~212 full_flag); decomposes as -42 (3D->2D in-order attn give-up, = surgical 357.6) + -133 (full matmul-tax machinery: cudagraph/TF32/aten overrides)

census_stepA_diagnostic_us.note: M=1 AR eager stepA latency, DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (#461: occupancy-noise-dominated, does NOT price the served M=8 verify lever). Corroborates that the aten-tax ALONE (byteexact_bi, attn kept) is near-free at M=1 (+1.6%); the big jump (+13.9%) appears only in fullenv, which also reverts attn->2D + TF32-off -> confirms the tax cost is full_flag MACHINERY, not a Marlin reduction-order cost

tps_cost_of_full_tax.fixed_order_gemm_recoverable_reason: int4 Marlin is already 0-flip M-invariant at M<=8 (microbench, all 5 deployed shapes); a fixed-split-K Marlin recovers 0 flips because there are 0 matmul-induced flips to recover. Marlin has no split-K Python knob; pinning split-SIZE needs a gptq_marlin.cu rebuild (out of scope). A forced bf16 fixed-order GEMM costs 2.6-2.7x (persistent_over_cublas).

reconciliation_with_500.pr500_claim: byteexact's 5 census flips are 'matmul-induced' -- they come from the un-taxed fast Marlin matmul (M-dependent), with the count set by how byteexact's 3D attention output lands on the M-dependent matmul knife-edges
reconciliation_with_500.pr518_correction: REFUTED. The 5 flips are an ATTENTION-reduction-order effect of the specific 3D fixed-order scheme, NOT matmul-induced.

decisive_controls.matmul_held_inert_attn_varies: byteexact_bi -> byteexact_fullenv both have aten_mm_bitexact=True (matmul tax ON in both); the ONLY difference is the attention scheme (3D fixed-order vs 2D in-order) -> flips 5 -> 1. The attention scheme moves the flip count.
decisive_controls.attn_held_matmul_varies: byteexact -> byteexact_bi both keep the 3D fixed-order attention (attn_is_batch_invariant=False); the ONLY difference is the aten matmul tax (off vs on) -> flips 5 -> 5, IDENTICAL flip set. The matmul tax moves the flip count by 0.
decisive_controls.marlin_m_invariant: microbench (j6dxoe28): int4 Marlin is 0-flip M-invariant at M<=8 on all 5 deployed shapes -> the production matmul is not the M-dependent reducer either.

reconciliation_with_500.why_500_was_imprecise: #500 over-relied on the 4-position #496 attention microbench reading 0/8 to conclude 'attention byte-exact -> flips must be matmul'. The 4 synthetic probe positions are necessary-but-not-sufficient: the e2e census exercises the full position distribution, where byteexact's 3D fixed-order reduction tree still differs from M=1-AR by 1 ULP at 5 positions (all near-ties, max margin 0.25 nat, 0 semantic). The matmul tax being inert + Marlin being M-invariant leave the attention scheme as the sole flip lever.
reconciliation_with_500.canonical_agreement: matches #461 (lmhead_only matmul tax divergence == deployed, 0 flip reduction; only attn 2D pin reduces flips) and #501 (Marlin M-invariant, no split-K knob). This census reproduces that pattern first-party on the byteexact base.

literal_rung_verdict.reason: There is no configuration that is BOTH literal (census <=1 flip) AND faster than surgical-357. (a) The matmul is not the flip source (tax inert 5->5; Marlin M-invariant). (b) The only <=1-flip config is byteexact_fullenv = revert attention to 2D in-order = surgical's exact attention scheme, which gives up byteexact's entire +42 TPS 3D-parallelism lever AND (with the full env tax) drops to ~212 < surgical 357.6. So 'literal byteexact' is strictly slower than the existing surgical-357 literal rung.
literal_rung_verdict.byteexact_quality_safety: 0 semantic flips (all 5 are bf16-ULP near-ties, max margin 0.25 nat); served self-determinism r1-r2=1.0 (#500); PPL 2.3767 <= 2.42. Quality-safe under the official PPL+completion gate.
literal_rung_verdict.fullenv_1_flip_not_a_subset: byteexact_fullenv's single flip (gpqa_diamond-21b950759e) is NOT one of byteexact's 5 -- the 2D in-order scheme closes byteexact's 5 but opens 1 different knife-edge (churn, exactly as #461 flagged). 2D in-order is tighter (1<5) but they are different flips; this is inherent argmax-near-tie churn, not a clean subset closure.
literal_rung_verdict.honest_floor: byteexact-399 stays a faster (+42 over surgical) byte-exact-ATTENTION + served-deterministic + quality-safe OPERATIVE candidate; it is NOT census-tight (literal). Fine under the quality framing. The literal/strict-byte-equivalent frontier rung remains surgical-357 (357.6, 1 flip). No fixed-split-K matmul lever closes the gap because the gap is not in the matmul.
